promotions.behavior_rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: No repo files were edited.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Repair invalid accepted JSON records before relying on a fresh graph-health run.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Read-only audit of graph health and provenance/source/chunk/claim lineage. Found graph health score reaches 0 because src/graph-health.ts uses a narrow source resolver that ignores accepted-record lineage fields used by writers, especially evidence.source and source_paths. Persisted graph-health artifact reported accepted_without_source_count=302, source_mapping_missing_count=302, score=0. Also found three accepted JSON records currently invalid, which can make a rerun fail before scoring.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Route task memories and behavior rules into candidates plus promotion review records before accepted retrieval.
In similar future work, apply this decision: No source files were edited per read-only audit scope.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat current Claude UserPromptSubmit support as installed-registration parity, not runtime-proof parity.

In similar future work, apply this decision: The most direct failing verifier should execute the Claude settings hook command with official Claude UserPromptSubmit JSON and assert Claude-labeled events/reports, which currently fail because the runtime hardcodes codex_prompt_submitted/codex_preflight_completed and codex_hook.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Add a Claude-specific hook verifier before claiming closed-loop proof for any Claude Code session.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Extend verify:os to include Claude hook command existence/execution and hook-disable policy checks when Claude prompt hook is required.

In similar future work, apply this decision: DinoBrain currently has a credible skeleton but does not yet satisfy the user's desired closed-loop LLM Wiki OS guarantees.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat hook wrapper fail-open paths, source lineage resolution, read-only/review semantics, and protected installer update behavior as P0 work before claiming OS completion.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat real dense hybrid retrieval, context/behavior evaluation, lifecycle backpressure, durable SQLite refresh, public/private sync split, Observatory read-trace correlation, and release parity as P1 work.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Critically audited DinoBrain against verified RAG knowledge and the user's desired OS using local code/data verification plus 10 subagent reviews. Produced and pushed a consensus audit report identifying P0/P1 improvements: outer wrapper fail-closed, real source/provenance lineage, true read-only/review mode, protected installer updates, real hybrid retrieval, lower-noise context eval, behavior eval, lifecycle backpressure, durable SQLite/cold memory, safer public sync, task-correlated Observatory, and release/version parity.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat current scalability as not complete while live wiki.sqlite, sqlite manifest, and dense-vectors.json are absent.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Recommend atomic or incremental wiki SQLite refresh instead of deleting the fast path and relying on manual rebuilds.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Recommend retrieval budgets and temperature/type-aware filtering so accepted behavior/task memories do not swamp Wiki/Source/Project knowledge.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Recommend FTS/prefix/vector top-K candidate generation and live performance canaries before claiming graph/LLM Wiki scale readiness.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Implement durable SQLite wiki refresh/upsert and manifest freshness checks.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completed read-only performance/scaling audit of DinoBrain retrieval, wiki index, SQLite shards, invalidation paths, verifier scripts, and live data scale. No code files edited. Findings: no P0; P1 risks are that the live wiki SQLite fast path is missing/non-durable after invalidation, cold/noisy accepted memory is indexed as normal hot retrieval input, and candidate generation still uses substring/wildcard scans plus lexical dense fallback rather than bounded semantic top-K retrieval. Current final live snapshot had no wiki-index.json, no sqlite manifest, no wiki.sqlite, no dense-vectors.json; operations.sqlite and operations-index were present. Accepted records were dominated by behavior rules.
Do not edit repository files during this audit.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat existing dinobrain-data local commits as protected user data; installer should stop rather than reset them.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat Codex hook trust as a manual security boundary; approval helper may guide but must not bypass trust.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Patch Checkout-DinoBrainRef/Sync-DinoBrainRepo to use ff-only update semantics and fail on local-ahead/diverged branches, especially for dinobrain-data.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Add release/version gates that require package version, installer metadata, tag, TargetCommitish, and published asset refs to agree before replacing a release asset.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completed read-only audit of DinoBrain installer/new PC/GitHub recovery/version parity/hook approval surfaces. Found one P0 risk: update/reinstall can strand unpushed local commits by force-resetting existing app/data branches with git checkout -B origin/ref. Found P1 gaps: Windows GUI blocks documented no-Git ZIP fallback; release/version parity can drift because package version, tag, setup metadata, and installer refs are not enforced together; uninstall removes Claude MCP but leaves the installed Claude UserPromptSubmit hook. Hook approval design itself preserves manual trust and has verification coverage.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat the existing system as a strong foundation, not complete OS proof, because current evidence still lacks live trusted-prompt proof and shows degraded graph/source mapping state.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Rank live pre-response loop proof, provenance/source mapping, and fast honest retrieval/index freshness as the top consensus blockers.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep the audit read-only with no source edits, no lifecycle application, and no git sync.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Kept repository source read-only; no code or data files were manually edited by the audit.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Used DinoBrain protocol context pack and primary RAG guidance sources for comparison.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Treated graph-health score 0/degraded as honest status, but incomplete lineage coverage remains a blocking graph-health issue.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completed read-only audit of DinoBrain durable source/provenance/source-chunk-claim lineage and graph health. Findings: graph health is correctly degraded but reports all accepted instances as missing source mapping; current source/provenance records exist for RAG guidance, but accepted-memory lineage recognition only checks narrow single-source fields and misses source_paths/source_chunk_path/claim_paths/provenance records. The graph index currently models record/root/folder/tag/kind/wikilink edges, not the provenance/lifecycle/quarantine/usage edges required by the completion contract. Node/npm were unavailable in the shell, so verification was static plus direct file inspection rather than npm verifier execution.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat the current behavior-eval implementation as retrieval-path proof, not Ragas/LangSmith-style behavior proof.
Do not claim real-vault memory-on behavior improvement until representative behavior-golden cases and answer-quality scoring exist.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Memory-use audit needs hard pass/fail semantics for expected memory and observed evidence before it can serve as proof.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Tighten audit_memory_use so missing expected memory, hallucinated references, and unverified artifacts can fail proof checks.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completed read-only audit of behavior evaluation, feedback writeback, memory audit, memory-on/off proof, and verify-v2 behavior proof surfaces. Found that current behavior evaluation is retrieval/term scoring rather than answer-quality or correction-behavior proof, verify-v2 uses only a temporary self-referential golden case, and memory-use audit returns ok even when trust evidence is degraded.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat exact user-provided RAG URLs as directly verified summary sources, not merely anchor-only backlog.
Do not store raw full external pages; store bounded paraphrased claims with source URLs and provenance links.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Accept core RAG, hybrid retrieval, reranking, source/chunk/claim lineage, GraphRAG overlay, and evaluation guidance into DinoBrain memory.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Hold paper/provider-specific claims unless source-bound and refreshed; reject overgeneralizations such as RAG guaranteeing truth or lexical fallback equaling dense hybrid retrieval.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep current live retrieval mode labeled lexical_fallback_v2 until a real embedding provider is configured.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Implement or configure a real dense embedding provider before claiming live dense hybrid retrieval.
In similar future work, apply this decision: DinoBrain evaluation should separate retrieval-component quality from end-to-end behavior proof.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Embedding benchmarks such as MTEB and STS should be used as context, not as substitutes for DinoBrain-specific retrieval and behavior evaluation.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Add hard-negative retrieval cases and source-provenance checks before trusting semantic similarity scores.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Accept high-purity RAG architecture, retrieval quality, hybrid retrieval, reranking placement, indexing/query-time separation, and provenance/evaluation guidance as durable DinoBrain knowledge.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Hold Lewis RAG details and managed cloud KB feature claims as paper/provider-specific unless source-bound records are created.

Use official Microsoft GraphRAG docs and Microsoft Research posts as primary evidence.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Frame graph retrieval as an added retrieval layer for cross-document synthesis, holistic themes, entity-neighborhood queries, and source lineage, not as a blanket replacement for vector/snippet retrieval.

Use provider rerank scores for within-query ordering, not as globally calibrated relevance probabilities.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Implemented and ran the real Codex session knowledge promotion step. Added a reusable session:promote command that reads local Codex JSONL sessions, extracts repeated preferences/decisions/mistakes/rules via deterministic pattern aggregation, and writes public-safe paraphrased accepted memory plus review records without storing raw transcripts, message text, previews, or absolute local session paths. Ran it against 47 non-empty local Codex sessions / 4477 messages and promoted 15 accepted codex-session-knowledge memories plus a Codex Session Knowledge Profile and promotion reports. Rebuilt Wiki/SQLite indexes; wiki_search retrieves the promoted profile from sqlite_shards. Strengthened public-data safety to scan untracked sync candidates as well as tracked files; public-data safety reports degraded but blocker_count=0. App changes were committed and pushed as 5582bfe.

Added a reusable RAG source-anchor seeding command and seeded user-provided RAG methodology anchors as anchor_only_unverified source/provenance candidates, plus a RAG Methodology Anchor Catalog. In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion requires live pre-response OS enforcement, fail-closed gates, closed task lifecycle, honest hybrid retrieval, bounded/index-backed candidate generation, graph health, lifecycle cleanup, source provenance, behavior eval, memory-use audit, feedback writeback, Observatory evidence, privacy/public-data safety, new-PC recovery equivalence, and release/sync hygiene.

Do not claim DinoBrain OS completion until the mandatory gates and proof registry are satisfied against live current data.

In similar future work, apply this decision: DISAGREE that scalability completion is met while wiki.sqlite, manifest.json, and dense-vectors.json are absent from the live data index state.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion requires bounded candidate selection and latency budgets; current verifiers measure elapsed time but do not fail on agreed performance thresholds.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion requires graph health freshness against the current accepted corpus and graph adjacency/index metadata, not a stale healthy record.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion requires cold data compaction/archive boundaries so raw sessions, unreviewed candidates, old tasks/traces/context packs, and broad accepted behavior rules do not dominate default retrieval.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Create or refresh live .dino/index/sqlite/wiki.sqlite and .dino/index/sqlite/manifest.json, then verify get_context_pack and wiki_search report candidate_source=sqlite_shards_v2 on the real data repo.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Define hot/warm/cold retention: raw sessions stay local-only and excluded, operational logs are partitioned/archived by time/project, and accepted behavior memories are type-budgeted so they do not swamp durable Wiki/Source/Project records.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion Reviewer 10 completed a read-only data architecture/performance review across dinobrain and dinobrain-data. Conclusion: AGREE with the intended index-first, SQLite-sharded, graph-aware, provenance-aware direction, but DISAGREE that the current system is scalability-complete. Current live data has operations.sqlite tracked/present, but no wiki.sqlite, no SQLite manifest, no dense-vectors index, and the persisted graph-health is stale/false healthy relative to the accepted corpus. Synthetic verifiers pass but report timings instead of enforcing latency budgets. Completion should require fresh wiki+operations SQLite shards, bounded indexed candidate selection, real BM25/dense retrieval constraints, graph adjacency freshness/health gates, cold-data archive/compaction policy, and explicit latency/freshness gates that fail when the prompt path regresses toward full-vault scans.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Observatory completion must be defined by visible operational truth, not only a passing graph UI fixture.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Graph-health freshness must be a gate; the current persisted graph-health file is stale relative to the wiki index and accepted corpus.

In similar future work, apply this decision: The live graph should prioritize semantic clusters, search, hover detail, and state visibility, and avoid dinosaur/fossil/decorative visual metaphors as completion criteria.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Active tasks must distinguish actually-running work from stale started records and show blocked/pending/verifier/main lanes explicitly.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Memory-use visibility must show provided, declared-used, observed-used, missing expected, hallucinated references, trust score, and graph-health snapshot, not just an audit count.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Regenerate graph health after wiki/operations index updates and fail readiness when graph-health is older than the index or inconsistent with accepted record count.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Add/verify an Observatory real-data health card that compares persisted graph-health to a fresh sampled health computation and flags stale false-healthy states.
Do not treat accepted behavior memories as source-truth citations by default; they prove prior agent/task claims, not external factual correctness.

In similar future work, apply this decision: All currently inspected accepted records are source_status=internal and resolve to .dino/traces/*; this is acceptable for agent behavior memory but insufficient for external facts or public claims.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion should require real 30_Sources/chunks/* records plus .dino/provenance/* links for factual claims, external evidence, citations, and last_verified metadata.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Graph/provenance health is currently stale or incomplete: graph-health reports zero accepted instances while wiki-index contains the accepted corpus, so completion requires regenerated health metrics and failing checks for missing source chunks/provenance.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Trust scoring should remain an audit of provided/declared/observed memory use, not a truth score.

In similar future work, apply this decision: LLM Wiki completion must require fast indexed candidate selection, honest retrieval-mode reporting, and true dense retrieval only when a usable dense vector index/provider exists.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Operational traces/accepted behavior rules currently create ranking pressure; completion requires durable wiki/source/project knowledge to outrank broad operational behavior rules when the query intent calls for it.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Graph completion is not the visual graph alone; it requires fresh indexed nodes/edges, wikilinks, provenance/lifecycle edges, graph-health freshness, and degraded status when empty or stale.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Quality completion is not satisfied until representative context and behavior golden sets pass under growth/noise budgets, including semantic paraphrase, rare exact queries, provenance, quarantine, and recency cases.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Latency completion should require the prompt path to avoid full vault scans and use SQLite wiki plus operations shards with measured query and rebuild budgets as data grows.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Tune ranking and/or type-specific budgets so broad accepted behavior rules do not swamp durable wiki/source/project records.

In similar future work, apply this decision: v2 stability risk is mostly in fail-closed enforcement, automatic lifecycle execution, strict eval gating, and active-session MCP/tool reload guarantees.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Patch hook duplicate-lock path to inject fail-closed unless a concrete sibling preflight report/context can be read.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completed stability-focused QA/code review of DinoBrain v2 changes. Findings: Codex hook duplicate-lock path can skip preflight instead of fail-closing; Claude Code integration is MCP-only and does not enforce pre-response OS context; action-gate required-tool checks still cover only legacy tools; node lifecycle apply=true writes review records rather than executing merge/hold/delete/provenance repair; behavior eval exits ok when golden file is missing; Observatory JSON graph fallback rejects v2 wiki index when SQLite is unavailable; create_source_chunk stores raw chunk_text without redaction/size bounds; os_begin_task can leave started tasks if context-pack construction throws. Type check passed after adding portable Node directory to PATH; eval:behavior currently returns ok/skipped because behavior-golden.json is missing.
Use os_begin_task as the default v2 pre-response entrypoint for hooks and agents.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Fail closed when OS context is missing or destructive-risk gates block the request.
Use local deterministic hybrid retrieval: contextual chunks, BM25 sparse, dense-lite lexical similarity, RRF, provenance and root-intent reranking.
